DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF ANNA MAGELSSEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE NOTICE WAS INSUFFICIENT PER C.C.P. SECTIONS 1005 AND 1013. THE FIRST DAY THIS MOTION COULD HAVE BEEN HEARD IS MARCH 29, 2010. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAGELSSEN, ANNA MARIA HEARING SET FOR APR-30-2010 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPT 612
REMOVED FROM PRE-ARBITRATION SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR SET FOR APR-20-2010 AFTER HEARING; MATTER DID NOT SETTLE
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-30-2010 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 612

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF ANNA MAGELSSEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES BY DEFENDANT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $540, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. NO APPEARANCES, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF JUN-28-2010 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff ANNA MAGELSSEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories By Defendant is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $540, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

The notice was insufficient per C.C.P. Sections 1005 and 1013. The first day this motion could have been heard is March 29, 2010. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
